# logfile

```go
import "im/internal/logfile"
```

Package logfile manages daily log file creation, timestamp logic, and append.

## Constants

<a name="SubheadingLevel"></a>SubheadingLevel is the Markdown heading level used for timestamp subheadings.

```go
const SubheadingLevel = "##"
```

## Variables

<a name="timestampRe"></a>timestampRe matches a timestamp subheading like "\#\# 14:30".

```go
var timestampRe = regexp.MustCompile(`(?m)^` + SubheadingLevel + ` (\d{2}):(\d{2})\s*$`)
```

<a name="ParseLastTimestamp"></a>
## func ParseLastTimestamp

```go
func ParseLastTimestamp(content string) (time.Time, bool)
```

ParseLastTimestamp extracts the time from the last \#\# HH:MM heading in content. Returns the parsed time \(on a zero date\) and true, or zero time and false if no valid heading is found.

<a name="ShouldEmitHeading"></a>
## func ShouldEmitHeading

```go
func ShouldEmitHeading(strategy config.TimestampRounding, now, last time.Time, hasLast bool) (bool, time.Time)
```

ShouldEmitHeading determines whether a new timestamp subheading should be written, and if so, what time to display. Returns \(false, zero\) when no heading should be emitted.

<a name="dateHeading"></a>
## func dateHeading

```go
func dateHeading(t time.Time) string
```

dateHeading returns the level\-1 heading for a daily log file.

<a name="roundDown10"></a>
## func roundDown10

```go
func roundDown10(t time.Time) time.Time
```

roundDown10 truncates minutes to the nearest 10\-minute boundary.

```go
func shouldEmitAdaptive(now, last time.Time, hasLast bool) (bool, time.Time)
```

<a name="Appender"></a>
## type Appender

Appender appends formatted entries to daily log files.

```go
type Appender struct {
    clock    func() time.Time
    strategy config.TimestampRounding
}
```

<a name="NewAppender"></a>
### func NewAppender

```go
func NewAppender(clock func() time.Time, cfg config.Config) *Appender
```

NewAppender creates an Appender. The clock function is called to get the current time \(inject time.Now in production, a fixed clock in tests\).

<a name="Appender.Append"></a>
### func \(\*Appender\) Append

```go
func (a *Appender) Append(dir, body string, task bool) error
```

Append writes body to the daily log file in dir. If task is true, each line is prefixed with a Markdown checkbox.
